news.headline: WSP to release Q1 2025 results on May 7, 2025, annual meeting of shareholders on May 8, 2025 at 11:00 am ET.
news.summary: Montreal, April 02, 2025 - WSP Global Inc. (TSX: WSP), a top professional services company, is set to announce its first quarter results for 2025 on May 7, 2025, following the market's closure. A discussion regarding the results will take place via a conference call and webcast on May 8, 2025, at 8:00 a.m. ET. Additionally, WSP will conduct its yearly shareholders meeting on May 8, 2025, at 11:00 a.m. ET.

news.headline: Quorum to release Q4 and fiscal year 2024 results on April 16, 2025 after markets close. TSX-V: QIS.
news.summary: A software company, Quorum Information Technologies Inc. (TSX-V: QIS), based in Calgary, Alberta, is planning to unveil its Q4 and fiscal year 2024 financial results on Wednesday, April 16, 2025, after the market closes. Quorum specializes in providing vital enterprise solutions for automotive dealerships and Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) across North America. This announcement is eagerly anticipated by investors and industry analysts looking to assess the company's performance. Stay tuned for the upcoming release of crucial financial data.

news.headline: FTG Corp (TSX: FTG) to announce Q1 2025 earnings on April 9, 2025, after market closes.
news.summary: In Toronto on April 2, 2025, Firan Technology Group Corporation (TSX: FTG) (OTCQX: FTGFF), known as FTG, announced its plans to disclose its financial performance for the first quarter of 2025. The results are scheduled for release on Wednesday, April 9, 2025, after the market closes.

news.headline: Shattuck Labs will attend Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 7-10 to discuss novel therapies targeting TNF receptors.
news.summary: In Austin, Texas and Durham, North Carolina, on April 02, 2025, Shattuck Labs, Inc. (NASDAQ: STTK), a biotech firm focused on developing innovative treatments for patients with inflammatory and immune-related conditions by targeting TNF superfamily receptors, disclosed that its executives will engage in discussions and presentations at the 24th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference from April 7–10, 2025.

news.headline: Webcast of First Quarter 2025 Earnings Conference Call by CenterPoint Energy, Inc. scheduled.
Brown & Brown, Inc. will release its 2025 first-quarter earnings on April 28, 2025 and host a conference call on April 29 at 8:00 a.m. EDT.
news.summary: Daytona Beach, Florida - Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE: BRO) has announced that it is scheduled to unveil its first-quarter earnings report for 2025 on April 28, 2025, shortly after the market closes. Following this release, J. Powell Brown, the company's President, and CEO, along with R. Andrew Watts, Executive Vice President, and CFO, will conduct an investor update conference call on April 29, 2025. The conference call will focus on discussing Brown & Brown's financial performance during the first quarter of 2025. The event will be live-streamed on Brown & Brown's official website starting at 8:00 a.m. EDT. To tune in, visit www.bbrown.com, navigate to "Investor Relations," and select "Calendar of Events" to join the broadcast.

news.headline: Nexus Industrial REIT (TSX: NXR.UN) to release Q1 results on May 15, 2025, for quarter ending March 31, 2025.
news.summary: On April 2, 2025, Nexus Industrial REIT (TSX: NXR.UN) revealed plans to unveil its financial performance for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025, before the TSX opening on May 15, 2025.

news.headline: Silynxcom Ltd. closes public offering, sells 1,290,000 shares at $2.25 each, raising $2.9 million. Underwriters may buy additional 193,500 shares.
news.summary: Israeli company Silynxcom Ltd. (NYSE American: SYNX), specializing in ruggedized tactical communication headset devices and accessories, has successfully concluded its previously announced underwritten public offering. The offering involved 1,290,000 ordinary shares sold at $2.25 per share, resulting in gross proceeds of around $2.9 million. The Company granted underwriters a 45-day option to acquire up to an additional 193,500 ordinary shares at the same price to account for over-allotments, if any.

news.headline: Getty Images and Shutterstock get second request from U.S. DOJ for merger, (2nd Request) for additional information and material.
news.summary: Today, Getty Images Holdings, Inc. (NYSE: GETY) and Shutterstock, Inc. (NYSE: SSTK) disclosed that they have both been issued a Second Request by the U.S. Department of Justice concerning their merger. This request for additional information and documentation is part of the regulatory process and signifies that the DOJ is reviewing the proposed merger in depth. The companies are working to provide the necessary materials as the merger moves forward.

news.headline: Oportun secures $187.5M in warehouse facility, bringing total capacity to $954M.
news.summary: Raises the overall committed warehouse capacity to nearly $954 million.

news.headline: WF International Limited completed IPO, selling 1.4 million shares at $4 each, totaling $5.6 million. Shares started trading on NASDAQ as "WXM".
news.summary: Yesterday in CHENGDU, China, WF International Limited (NASDAQ: WXM), a company specializing in electromechanical solutions for HVAC systems, floor heating systems, and water purification systems, successfully completed its initial public offering. The company sold 1,400,000 ordinary shares at $4.00 per share, raising $5,600,000 in total gross proceeds before underwriting discounts and expenses. The shares began trading on the Nasdaq Capital Market on April 1, 2025, with the ticker symbol “WXM.”

news.headline: Motiva Port Arthur Expects to Restart FCC Within One Week
Record inflows for Canada ETFs due to market volatility in March.
'Mario Kart boosts sales of Nintendo Switch, driving optimism for 2-player mode.'

Fidelity now offers IRAs for crypto investing with minimal fees. No fees to open or maintain accounts, but 1% spread on buy/sell transactions. US crypto landscape evolving. Some IRA providers allowed crypto purchases; Fidelity's move indicates a shift. Options like BTC and ETH ETFs available since 2024. Rise of Bitcoin IRAs and companies like BitIRA catering to digital assets. Momentum in allowing crypto in retirement accounts seen with a bill by Alabama Senator Tuberville.
news.summary: Fidelity, a financial services company managing $5.9 trillion in assets, has launched new retirement accounts allowing Americans to invest in crypto fee-free. These include a tax-deferred traditional IRA and two Roth IRAs, with the option to buy Bitcoin, Ether, and Litecoin. Fidelity charges a 1% spread on crypto transactions, making the process easier for individuals. The majority of crypto holdings are kept in cold storage for security. Previously, few IRA providers permitted cryptocurrency purchases, indicating a shift in the landscape. Since 2024, ETFs have offered exposure to BTC and ETH in retirement accounts. The growing trend of including crypto in retirement accounts is evident, with Senator Tuberville reintroducing a bill to allow crypto in 401(k)s.

Trump imposes tariffs as high as 67%; EU faces 20% tariff; market sheds almost $700 billion.
news.summary: President Trump announced new tariffs as a way to rectify trade imbalances, introducing a reciprocal tariff rate of 50% based on what the US's trading partners charge, factoring in non-tariff barriers. The exceeding duty levels, up to mid-30% with key partners, led to a sell-off in after-hours trading. China, with tariffs up to 67%, will face 37% US duties, the EU with a 39% rate will see 20%, and Japan with 46% barriers will have a 24% duty. These measures aim to address trade disparities but have shaken investors. (Word count: 92)

news.headline: US Treasury Secretary Nominee Bessent: avoiding commentary on market

ETH price may have bottomed, but pro traders show little interest; at $1,768 low, it's struggled to reach $2,000. Year-to-date, Ether is 44% down with futures metrics indicating lack of bullish sentiment. Ether's dominance in DeFi remains strong despite DApps revenue drop. Whale hesitance seen in ETH options market, while retail trading enthusiasm wanes due to ETH ETFs. Overall cautious outlook on ETH price.
news.summary: Ether (ETH) has seen a 6.4% increase in price, but struggles to reach $2,000 due in part to the deflating memecoin market. Year-to-date, Ether is down by 44% with little bullish sentiment among traders. Premium on Ether futures is below the neutral 5% threshold, showing lack of confidence. The ETH options market indicates risk-aversion sentiment. Despite the decline in Ethereum DApps revenue, the network's adoption remains strong. Ethereum holds a high amount of stablecoins and TVL, suggesting potential for adoption. Despite challenges, the Ethereum network continues to expand. Retail traders have neutral sentiment towards Ether due to ETFs. The funding rate has been neutral since March 31. Derivatives data and sentiment indicate caution among traders and investors.

news.headline: D2L Inc. reports strong Q4 results with $53.3 million revenue, 12% YoY increase. Full-year revenue reached $205.3 million, 13% growth. Subscription and support revenue jump to $46.8 million in Q4, 11% growth YoY. Cash flow from operating activities rose to $27.9 million in Fiscal 2025, an increase of $12.2 million YoY. Adjusted EBITDA at $9.4 million in Q4. CEO expresses satisfaction with company's performance; anticipates continued growth and profitability for Fiscal 2026. Medium-term outlook expects 10-15% revenue growth and 18-20% Adjusted EBITDA margin by Fiscal 2028.
news.summary: D2L Inc., a global learning technology company, reported strong financial results for Q4 Fiscal 2025 and full year with total revenue increasing by 12% to US$53.3 million in Q4 and 13% to US$205.3 million for the full year. Q4 subscription and support revenue grew 11% to US$46.8 million, and full-year revenue in this segment increased by 11% to US$180.6 million. Constant Currency Annual Recurring Revenue reached US$205.3 million at year-end, up by 9%. Cash flow from operating activities in Fiscal 2025 was US$27.9 million, a significant increase. The Q4 Adjusted EBITDA was US$9.4 million, a notable increase from the prior year. D2L's customer base grew to over 1,430, and the company received various accolades for its learning platform. The company provided financial guidance for Fiscal 2026, predicting subscription and support revenue in the range of $194 million to $196 million, total revenue in the range of $219 million to $221 million, and Adjusted EBITDA in the range of $32 million to $34 million. This outlook reflects a balance between growth and profitability, highlighting continued investment for growth while scaling towards increased profitability.

news.headline: Specialty retailers Wayfair, Williams-Sonoma see shares drop after RH misses Q4 estimates with revenue at $812.41 million and EPS at $1.58 vs expected revenue of $829.56 million and EPS of $1.92, citing softening demand due to spiked mortgage rates.
news.summary: Specialty retailers in the furniture and home goods sector are experiencing a decline in after-hours trading on Wednesday due to disappointing earnings from RH (NYSE:RH). In the fourth quarter, RH reported revenue of $812.41 million, falling short of the estimated $829.56 million, with adjusted earnings of $1.58 per share compared to the expected $1.92 per share. The company attributed this underperformance to a decline in demand starting mid-December, coinciding with a spike in mortgage rates after the Federal Reserve indicated stability in rates. This commentary on softened demand can be found in the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: US Treasury Secretary Nominee Bessent learns to avoid watching activities in the after-hours market
RH (NYSE:RH) Q4 earnings disappoint with revenue of $812.41M missing $829.56M estimates & EPS of $1.58 missing $1.92. Total revenue up 10% YoY, 17% demand increase. Operating income $92M, up 38% YoY.
news.summary: RH (NYSE:RH) released their Q4 financial results, causing shares to drop due to a revenue miss. Their Q4 revenue stood at $812.41 million, falling short of the expected $829.56 million. Additionally, adjusted earnings per share were reported at $1.58, below the estimated $1.92 per share. Despite this, the company saw a 10% increase in total revenue compared to the previous year with a 17% surge in Q4 demand. RH also reported a 38% rise in adjusted operating income to $92 million. For more details, the full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Hong Kong hosts Arts celebration with global events showcasing art & culture, includes museum exhibitions and art auctions.
news.summary: The global celebration of art and culture known as Arts in Hong Kong recently took place, with various events held throughout the city. From museum exhibitions to art auctions, a wide range of activities were featured, highlighting the diverse artistic scene in Hong Kong.
